title: Profile
description: The candidate's facts — the only other source of truth besides Eval. This is a lean port of Career Ops' career.yml graph: 'fit + facts only' for v1 (adaptive-framing tables, negotiation scripts, and exit-narrative prose are deferred). The governing rule this schema exists to enforce: every 'bullets[].text' is verbatim, ready-to-use prose. Nothing downstream (gate, evaluate, resume, cover) may rewrite it — only select, reorder, and reframe around it. If a fact isn't here, it doesn't exist for tailoring purposes.

version.description: Bump this whenever facts change. Part of every downstream cache fingerprint — bumping it invalidates cached evals/resumes/covers so they regenerate against the new facts.

headline.description: One-line professional headline. Used as-is in resume rendering and as cheap context for the AI Gate.

targets.description: Role archetypes the candidate is open to, e.g. ['product-manager', 'apm', 'ai-agent-pm']. Drives Gate + Evaluate role_fit.

role_priorities.description: Ordered role-type preference, tier 1 (index 0) = highest priority, e.g. ['Product Manager', 'AI Product Manager', ...]. This is DATA the generic eval prompt reads to rank role_fit/seniority_fit — the engine has no hardcoded role names, so a different profile (e.g. a Software Engineer's) drives the same prompt toward entirely different priorities. Role fit stays an AI reasoning task; this is guidance, never a keyword filter.

ranking_notes.description: Free-text nuance the eval prompt applies when scoring role_fit/seniority_fit against role_priorities — e.g. how to treat a step-down in seniority, what makes a 'growth' role vs a title match, when a lower-tier role should still rank highly because of company quality. Optimizes for the candidate's stated long-term goal, not raw job count.

work_mode_priority.description: Ordered work-mode preference, tier 1 (index 0) = highest, e.g. ['global_remote', 'india_remote', 'navi_mumbai_onsite', 'mumbai_onsite']. A RANKING signal (affects ordering/logistics scoring), not a filter — the only work-mode FILTER is the onsite-city hard constraint in deal_breakers/location.

onsite_outside.description: Cities where on-site/hybrid is acceptable. On-site elsewhere without visa sponsorship is a hard logistics penalty in Evaluate.

min_years_ok.description: Below this JD-stated years requirement, it's a legitimate target, not a downlevel.

target_lpa.description: [min, max] target annual comp in lakhs per annum (or swap units freely — this is just a number pair).

floor_lpa.description: Hard reject floor — see careeros/pipeline/constraints.py. Only enforced when a job's salary is confidently known; unknown salary never rejects.

preferred_lpa.description: At/above this, comp is a positive signal in evaluation ranking (not a hard constraint, unlike floor_lpa).

summary_variants.description: Pre-written Professional Summary paragraphs. The resume prompt SELECTS one by matching jd_tags — it never composes a new one. Port of career.yml's narrative.summary_variants.

text.description: Exact, ready-to-use prose. Copied verbatim into the resume, never edited.

jd_tags.description: Empty array = generic, matches any JD.

experience.description: Reverse-chronological work history. Port of career.yml's companies[].

text.description: Exact, ready-to-use CV sentence. This is the ONLY thing a resume-generation prompt may place on the page for this fact — verbatim, never rephrased.

tags.description: JD-relevance tags used to rank/select this bullet for a given job.

visibility.description: headline = lead with it; supporting = include if there's room; hidden = true but not surfaced by default.
